title: HLW-001: Bad news
description:
AUTHOR: Anonymous

Bad news. The security daemon doesn't just use a static shelf number anymore. It rotates the physical bypass tunnel every 24 hours based on the server's daily diagnostic logs.

I intercepted the interface architecture. You need to write a class named ShelfOverride that implements the ShelfController interface.

The system will pass an array of integers into your method. The correct shelf number is the total count of diagnostic codes in that array that are BOTH strictly greater than 100 AND a multiple of 3.

YOUR TARGET:

Implement: ShelfController

Required Method: public int getTargetShelf(int[] diagnostics)

Write the loop. Filter the array. Return the final count.

Warning: Do not write an infinite loop. The server will time out, flag this terminal, and lock us out forever.

title: HLW-002: The encrypted gatekeeper is monitoring heat sig...
description:
AUTHOR: Ghost_Protocol

The encrypted gatekeeper is monitoring heat signatures. To bypass the thermal lock, you need to identify "critical spikes."

Write a class ThermalBypass that implements SensorArray. Iterate through the input array and count how many values are negative OR above 500.

TARGET:

Implement: SensorArray

Method: public int countSpikes(int[] heat)

title: HLW-003: We’re intercepting a packet stream
description:
AUTHOR: NullPointer



We’re intercepting a packet stream. The bridge will only open if we provide the sum of all "parity-aligned" IDs.



Implement BridgeProtocol. Your method must sum all even numbers in the array. If the array is empty, return 0.



TARGET:


Implement: BridgeProtocol


Method: public int getParitySum(int[] ids)

title: HLW-004: The vault uses a "Range-Lock" mechanism
description:
AUTHOR: Anonymous



The vault uses a "Range-Lock" mechanism. We need to verify if the server's sequence contains at least three values within the "Safe Zone" (between 10 and 20 inclusive).



Implement VaultUnlocker. Return true if the count of valid numbers is 3 or more, otherwise return false.



TARGET:


Implement: VaultUnlocker


Method: public boolean isSafe(int[] seq)

title: HLW-005: The watchdog timer is scanning for anomalies
description:
AUTHOR: Root_Access



The watchdog timer is scanning for anomalies. An anomaly is any value that is exactly double the value of the number immediately preceding it in the array.



Implement WatchdogBypass. Return the total count of these anomalies. Start your check from the second element.



TARGET:


Implement: WatchdogBypass


Method: public int findAnomalies(int[] stream)

title: HLW-006: Security Protocol is failing to compile
description:
Security Protocol is failing to compile. The security protocol uses the Decryptor interface, but the Clue class is missing the required method to unlock it.

TARGET: Write the missing method. The interface requires getShiftKey() to return an integer of the bypass shift key. The bypass shift key for this node is the amount of bookshelves added to the amount of student's desk.

title: HLW-007: The server room is overheating
description:
The server room is overheating. The CoolantSystem interface requires a getTemperatureThreshold() method to initiate the purge.



TARGET: Calculate the threshold by adding the roomCount (12) to the serverDensity (14).

title: HLW-008: The satellite dish is misaligned
description:
The satellite dish is misaligned. The Uplink interface requires a getSignalFrequency() method to lock onto the DevScape core.



TARGET: Calculate the frequency by multiplying the dishRotation (45) by the amplifierGain (2).

title: HLW-009: The archive vault is locked behind a syntax error
description:
The archive vault is locked behind a syntax error. The Vault interface requires getAccessKey() to verify the user.



TARGET: The access key is the vaultID (1024) minus the securityDelay (24).

title: HLW-010: Don't bother running straight to the lobby
description:
AUTHOR: Anonymous

Don't bother running straight to the lobby. The final exit keypad is completely powered down. The system won't supply power to the keypad until all five auxiliary security nodes in this hallway are decrypted.

The lockdown AI completely wiped the Firewall class from the server memory to stop us from getting the last clue. It only left the Mainframe interface intact.

TARGET: 
Rebuild the missing class from scratch.

Create a class named Firewall that implements Mainframe.

Implement the missing decrypt() method.

The method must scan the data array. If it finds the exact integer 404, it must return: getPasskeyRiddle()

If it doesn't find it, return "LOCKED".

title: HLW-011: The biometric scanner for the armory is offline
description:
AUTHOR: Cipher

The biometric scanner for the armory is offline. The auxiliary power routing needs a massive surge to bypass the mechanical breaker. We need to channel all available data packets and check if the total voltage is high enough.

TARGET:
Rebuild the missing routing class.

Create a class named PowerRouter that implements Mainframe.

Implement the missing decrypt() method.

The method must calculate the total sum of all integers in the data array. If the total is greater than 9000, it must return: getPasskeyRiddle()

If it isn't, return "LOCKED".

title: HLW-012: A logic bomb has been planted in the ventilatio...
description:
AUTHOR: Ghost



A logic bomb has been planted in the ventilation control subnet. We don't have time to sanitize the whole thing. We just need to find the trigger sequence before it executes. The bomb's signature is two consecutive negative integers.



TARGET:


Construct the scanner class.



Create a class named LogicScanner that implements Mainframe.



Implement the missing decrypt() method.



The method must scan the data array for two negative numbers in a row (e.g., -5 immediately followed by -1). If it finds this signature, return: getPasskeyRiddle()



If it reaches the end without finding the signature, return "LOCKED".

title: HLW-013: The main elevator requires a secure handshake t...
description:
AUTHOR: Overwatch

The main elevator requires a secure handshake to descend to the lab level. The encryption parity is strict: every single packet in the handshake sequence must be an even number. One odd packet means the connection is compromised.

TARGET:

Build the verification class.

Create a class named ParityCheck that implements Mainframe.

Implement the missing decrypt() method.

The method must evaluate the data array. If it detects ANY odd integer, immediately return: "LOCKED"

If it successfully scans the entire array and all numbers are even, return: getPasskeyRiddle()
